_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Scott County, KY. Formed 1792, same year as Kentucky statehood. Records begin from formation. No major courthouse fire. Georgetown College archives provide supplementary records.

previous_courthouses.fate: Replaced by current building. No significant fire.

record_losses.description: Scott County has no record of a major courthouse fire. Records run continuously from the 1792 formation.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} was in Scott County after 1792, the records survive.
record_losses.source: Kentucky Department for Libraries and Archives. Guide to Kentucky County Court Records.

wills_estates.gaps: Minor gaps in early loose papers.

vital_records.repository: Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services; KDLA

substitute_sources.description: Georgetown College (Baptist, founded 1829) maintained enrollment records, alumni records, and institutional papers that document Scott County families.
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Identifies families in the Bluegrass Baptist community. Enrollment records place individuals at specific dates.
substitute_sources.source: Harrison and Klotter, A New History of Kentucky (1997).

substitute_sources.description: Scott County's earliest settlers held Virginia land grants. Pre-statehood land records are in Virginia files.
substitute_sources.repository: Virginia Land Office, Library of Virginia; KDLA
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Links earliest settlers to Virginia origins and military service.

narrative_hooks: Scott County's records start with Kentucky statehood — if {ancestor_name} was here after 1792, the paper trail is clean.
Georgetown College records document Baptist families that the courthouse may not distinguish — if {ancestor_name} was connected to the college, the institutional records add depth.
Before statehood, {ancestor_name}'s Scott County land was granted under Virginia law — the Virginia Land Office holds the warrants.
Scott County's courthouse escaped every fire and every war — the records are as complete as any in the Bluegrass.
source_references: Kentucky Department for Libraries and Archives. Guide to Kentucky County Court Records.
Harrison, Lowell H., and James C. Klotter. A New History of Kentucky. Lexington: University Press of Kentucky, 1997.
Jillson, Willard Rouse. The Kentucky Land Grants. Louisville: Filson Club Publications, 1925.
